description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) configuration template for documentation generation agent

github.description: GitHub integration - fetch issues, PRs, discussions

figma.description: Figma integration - fetch designs and component details

jira.description: Jira integration - fetch tickets and specs

confluence.description: Confluence integration - fetch specs and background

custom-internal-api.description: Custom internal API - extend with your own MCP

github.step1: Create a personal access token at https://github.com/settings/tokens
github.step3: Copy token and set GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable

figma.step3: Copy token and set FIGMA_TOKEN environment variable
figma.step4: Find your team ID in Figma URL (figma.com/team/{TEAM_ID})

jira.step4: Update host and project_key in config

confluence.step1: Same as Jira - use same API token
confluence.step4: Update host to your Confluence URL

drawio.step1: This is a built-in optional MCP
drawio.step3: No auth required for basic usage

custom-sse-mcp.step1: Custom MCPs use Server-Sent Events (SSE) pattern
custom-sse-mcp.step2: Implement an HTTP server at your endpoint
custom-sse-mcp.step3: Implement SSE endpoints for agent communication
custom-sse-mcp.step4: See examples/ folder for starter templates

dotenv_file.description: Create .env file in workspace root (add to .gitignore)

testing.test_figma: Run: copilot-agent test --mcp figma --cmd 'fetch design figma-url'
testing.verify_all: All MCPs should respond within 2 seconds

example1_github_issue.description: Fetch issue and generate documentation from it
example1_github_issue.flow: agent → fetch issue #456 → analyze context → generate guide

example2_figma_design.description: Use Figma design to document UI feature
example2_figma_design.query: Document the auth flow shown in Figma design
example2_figma_design.flow: agent → fetch design → extract UI labels → generate steps

example3_combined.query: Document new dashboard feature from GitHub issue #123 and Figma design
example3_combined.flow: agent → fetch issue + design → combine context → generate guide with screenshots

customization_tips.tip1: Start with GitHub enabled (required for most workflows)
customization_tips.tip2: Add Figma if your team uses design-first documentation
customization_tips.tip3: Enable Jira/Confluence if integrated with issue tracking
customization_tips.tip4: Custom MCPs allow integration with internal tools (wikis, DBs, etc.)
customization_tips.tip5: Use .env file for sensitive tokens (never commit to git)
